channel.title: Thiccctionary
channel.description: A satirical daily dictionary of objects of unusual girth. Strictly things, never people.

item.title: [Article] The Bagger 288: The Thiccc-est Machine on Earth
item.description: At 96 metres tall and 220 metres long, the Bagger 288 is the largest land vehicle ever built. A field report on what it means to be truly, structurally, irreversibly thiccc.

item.title: Bagger 288
item.description: A leviathan of earth-moving machinery, noteworthy for its massive revolving bucket wheel that shovels earth with an unhurried inevitability; esp. one observed from a distance, silhouetted against the horizon as an engineering colossus.

item.title: [Article] Foods of Substance: A Thiccc Survey
item.description: From Parmigiano wheels to heritage tomatoes to Atlantic Giant pumpkins — when food's structural commitment becomes its identity.

item.title: Spruce, Suburban
item.description: A mature evergreen specimen (genus Picea) cultivated as a residential or municipal landscape feature; characteristically pyramidal, with branches sweeping low to the ground in a dense conical mass.

item.title: Hoover Dam
item.description: A concrete arch-gravity dam spanning the Black Canyon of the Colorado River, completed 1936; 726 feet from foundation to crest, 660 feet of base thickness tapering to 45 feet at the crown.

item.title: Wheel, Parmigiano-Reggiano
item.description: A cylindrical mass of aged cheese, notable for its robust curvature and dense, granular interior; often showcased in markets and delicatessens worldwide.

item.title: Watermelon, Moon and Stars
item.description: A cultivar of watermelon renowned for its celestial patterning of yellow spots and larger orbs set against a dark green rind, reminiscent of a night sky.

item.title: [Article] The Anatomy of a Thiccc Vehicle
item.description: Five parts worth knowing: wheelbase, aft, dually, cargo bay, kin. A field guide to the parts of a thiccc vehicle.

item.title: [Article] Concrete: A Material Study
item.description: The material behind cement mixers, Brutalism, and Hoover Dam. Why concrete forces thicccness on everything it builds.

item.title: Sofa, Chesterfield
item.description: A capacious upholstered seating apparatus characterized by its deep button-tufting and rolled arms; esp. one whose presence becomes a room's anchor furniture by physical fact.

item.title: [Article] Thiccc Things You Walk Past Every Day
item.description: Fire hydrants, mailboxes, bollards, dumpsters, HVAC — the unsung architecture of a sidewalk.

item.title: [Article] A Taxonomy of Thiccc Architecture
item.description: Defense, ideology, and mass housing. Three programs, one silhouette. Castle walls, Brutalism, Khrushchyovka.

item.title: [Article] The Inherent Thicccness of Industrial Scale
item.description: Why physics insists on the silhouette. Bagger 288, Saturn V, supertankers, Hoover Dam.

item.title: Banana, Cavendish
item.description: A cultivar of banana (Musa acuminata) renowned for its robust and curvaceous physique, often found lounging in fruit bowls and breakfast spreads across the globe.

item.title: [Article] Why Cement Trucks Are the Thiccc-est Industrial Vehicle
item.description: An argument, with engineering. The mixer is not in competition. It is the standard.

item.title: [Article] How to Spot a Thiccc Thing in the Wild: A Field Guide
item.description: Three tests. No instruments required. Silhouette, ratio, character.

item.title: Frigidaire, Side-by-Side
item.description: A domestic paragon of capacious elegance, featuring dual chilled chambers that stand in ostentatious parallel, prepared to preserve victuals with all the subtlety of a colossus.

item.title: [Article] The Five Thiccc-est Things We’ve Catalogued (So Far)
item.description: A working ranking. Heritage tomatoes to wide-body airliners. A hierarchy of girth.

item.title: [Article] What Counts as Thiccc? An Editorial Guide
item.description: The standing reference for what we’ll catalog and what we won’t. Includes the submission test.

item.title: [Article] A Brief History of Thiccc, the Word
item.description: From early-2000s message boards to mainstream dictionaries. With sources.

item.title: Thiccc Boeing
item.description: A widebody aircraft whose aft fuselage exhibits significantly more curvature than its fore fuselage; esp. one observed tail-on, in long-lens profile.

item.title: Avocado, Domestic
item.description: A pear-shaped fruit (Persea americana) of disproportionate top-to-bottom mass distribution, often photographed sliced for visual emphasis.

item.title: Ram 3500, Dually
item.description: A heavy-duty pickup truck of substantial posterior breadth, distinguished by dual rear wheels per side and an aggressively flared bedside that reads as architectural; esp. one photographed in golden-hour parking-lot stillness.

item.title: Heritage Tomato
item.description: A cultivar of tomato, often grown from seeds passed down through generations, characterized by its robust flavor and generous proportions.

item.title: Concrete Mixer
item.description: An industrial vehicle of contemplative rotundity, characterized by a single, slowly-rotating, drum-shaped midsection.

item.title: Pumpkin, Atlantic Giant
item.description: A cultivar of pumpkin renowned for its prodigious girth, cultivated primarily for competitive size and decorative impact.
